title: श्रीनाथजी आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्रीनाथजी आरती ॥
॥ नाथद्वारा परम्परा ॥

जय श्रीनाथजी प्रभु, जय श्रीनाथजी।
गोवर्धनधारी प्रभु, जय श्रीनाथजी॥

मुखारविन्द मनोहर, नयन विशाला।
मस्तक मुकुट मणि, कुण्डल रसाला।
गले वैजयन्ती माला, हीरा मोतिन माला।
श्रीनाथजी प्रभु वदन कमला॥
जय श्रीनाथजी प्रभु॥ १॥

बाँया हाथ गोवर्धन, दाहिने हाथ कमला।
कमर में करधनी, पाँव में नूपुर बाला।
वंशी वट वृक्ष तले, गैयन संग लीला।
नन्दनन्दन गोपाला, यशोदा का लाला॥
जय श्रीनाथजी प्रभु॥ २॥

षड् ऋतु में षड् उत्सव, मंगला झाँकी।
श्रृंगार श्रीविग्रह, राजभोग थाली।
उत्थापन संध्या आरती, शयन प्यारी।
आठों पहर सेवा, वल्लभकुल दुलारी॥
जय श्रीनाथजी प्रभु॥ ३॥

वल्लभाचार्य प्रभु ने, प्रगट किया यहाँ।
पुष्टिमार्ग का सिद्धान्त, प्रेम भक्ति जहाँ।
नाथद्वारा निवासी, कृपा करो तहाँ।
भक्त वत्सल श्रीनाथजी, सुनो अरज यहाँ॥
जय श्रीनाथजी प्रभु॥ ४॥

दूध दही घृत माखन, भोग लगाओ।
तुलसी विलास कुञ्ज, शीतल जल पाओ।
गोपी गोप सखा सब, रास रचाओ।
श्रीनाथजी चरण सेवा, मुक्ति मन भाओ॥
जय श्रीनाथजी प्रभु, जय श्रीनाथजी।
गोवर्धनधारी प्रभु, जय श्रीनाथजी॥ ५॥

॥ इति श्रीनाथजी आरती सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrinathji Aarti ||
|| Nathdwara Parampara ||

Jaya Shrinathji Prabhu, Jaya Shrinathji.
Govardhana-dhari Prabhu, Jaya Shrinathji.

Mukharavinda Manohara, Nayana Vishala.
Mastaka Mukuta Mani, Kundala Rasala.
Gale Vaijayanti Mala, Hira Motina Mala.
Shrinathji Prabhu Vadana Kamala.
Jaya Shrinathji Prabhu. ||1||

Banya Hatha Govardhana, Dahine Hatha Kamala.
Kamara Mein Karadhani, Panva Mein Nupura Bala.
Vanshi Vata Vriksha Tale, Gaiyan Sanga Lila.
Nandanandana Gopala, Yashoda Ka Lala.
Jaya Shrinathji Prabhu. ||2||

Shad Ritu Mein Shad Utsava, Mangala Jhanki.
Shringara Shrivigraha, Rajabhoga Thali.
Utthapana Sandhya Aarti, Shayana Pyari.
Athon Pahara Seva, Vallabhakula Dulari.
Jaya Shrinathji Prabhu. ||3||

Vallabhacharya Prabhu Ne, Pragata Kiya Yahan.
Pushtimarga Ka Siddhanta, Prema Bhakti Jahan.
Nathdwara Nivasi, Kripa Karo Tahan.
Bhakta Vatsala Shrinathji, Suno Araja Yahan.
Jaya Shrinathji Prabhu. ||4||

Dudh Dahi Ghrit Makhana, Bhoga Lagao.
Tulasi Vilasa Kunja, Shitala Jala Pao.
Gopi Gopa Sakha Sab, Rasa Rachao.
Shrinathji Charana Seva, Mukti Mana Bhao.
Jaya Shrinathji Prabhu, Jaya Shrinathji.
Govardhana-dhari Prabhu, Jaya Shrinathji. ||5||

|| Iti Shrinathji Aarti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: गोवर्धनधारी श्रीनाथजी की जय! मनोहर मुखारविन्द, विशाल नयन, मस्तक पर मणि-मुकुट, कुण्डल, वैजयन्ती माला — कमलवत वदन। बायें हाथ में गोवर्धन, दाहिने हाथ में कमल, कमर में करधनी, पैरों में नूपुर। वंशीवट के नीचे गैय्यों के संग लीला करते नन्दनन्दन गोपाल। छह ऋतुओं में छह उत्सव — मंगला, शृंगार, राजभोग, उत्थापन, सन्ध्या, शयन — आठों पहर सेवा वल्लभकुल की। वल्लभाचार्य ने पुष्टिमार्ग प्रगट किया। नाथद्वारा निवासी भक्तवत्सल श्रीनाथजी कृपा करें। चरण-सेवा से मुक्ति मन को भाती है।
benefits: Sacred aarti of Shrinathji at Nathdwara — the foremost Pushti Marg shrine
Invokes Krishna as the child who lifted Govardhan to protect devotees
Connects the devotee to the rich Pushti Marg tradition of Vallabhacharya
Describes the eight-fold daily seva (ashta-chaap) of Shrinathji
Deepens prema-bhakti (love-devotion) which is the essence of Pushti Marg
Regular recitation brings Krishna's nurturing grace and material-spiritual abundance
description: The Shrinathji Aarti, known in Sanskrit as श्रीनाथजी आरती, is a traditional worship song (aarti) in praise of Lord Vitthal. This sacred composition is sourced from Nathdwara Pushti Marg Tradition (Vallabhacharya Sampradaya) and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to describe the eight-fold daily seva (ashta-chaap) of Shrinathji as well as regular recitation brings Krishna's nurturing grace and material-spiritual abundance. Additionally, it is known to invoke Krishna as the child who lifted Govardhan to protect devotees. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Janmashtami, Annakut, and Govardhan Puja. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Shrinathji Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Shrinathji Aarti with Hindi meaning. Nathdwara Pushti Marg aarti of Govardhana-dhari Krishna with transliteration.
relatedSearches: aarti shrinathji ni mangla kari lyrics

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shrinathji Aarti and which deity does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shrinathji Aarti, popularly known as 'Aarti Shrinathji Ni Mangla Kari', praises Shrinathji — the seven-year-old (Bal-svarupa) form of Lord Krishna who lifted Govardhan hill. The presiding deity of Nathdwara temple in Rajasthan, Shrinathji is the chief swarup of the Pushtimarg sampradaya founded by Shri Vallabhacharya in the early 16th century.

seoFaqs.question: What are the eight darshans at Nathdwara temple?
seoFaqs.answer: Shrinathji is offered eight daily darshans (Ashta Sakha sequence): Mangla (dawn awakening), Shringar (adornment), Gwal (cowherd play), Rajbhog (royal meal), Utthapan (afternoon rising), Bhog (evening snack), Sandhya (twilight) and Shayan (night rest). Each darshan has its own dedicated aarti and kirtan, with Mangla and Shayan being the most attended by pilgrims.

seoFaqs.question: What does the Pushtimarg tradition teach about the Shrinathji Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: Pushtimarg (the Path of Grace) treats the aarti as seva — loving service to Krishna as one's own beloved child. The aarti is sung in Vraj Bhasha and Gujarati rather than Sanskrit, reflecting Vallabhacharya's principle that intimate love-devotion (sneh-bhakti) needs no Vedic ritual mediation. The lamp is waved, conch and bell are sounded, and devotees take darshan as Shrinathji 'wakes' or 'rests'.

seoFaqs.question: Where is Shrinathji Aarti performed and when can pilgrims attend?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal Shrinathji Aarti is performed at the Shrinathji Temple in Nathdwara, Rajasthan — about 50 km from Udaipur. The temple is built like a haveli with seven flags representing the Pushtimarg sect. Mangla aarti happens around 5:30 AM and Shayan around 7 PM (timings shift seasonally). Janmashtami, Annakut (Govardhan Puja), and Holi see the largest crowds.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Shrinathji Aarti different from a standard Krishna Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: Standard Krishna aartis like 'Aarti Kunj Bihari Ki' are sung in Hindi/Braj across the Vaishnava world. The Shrinathji Aarti is specifically Pushtimargiya — composed in Vraj-Gujarati, performed only for the Govardhan-lifting bal-svarupa, and tied to the eight-darshan seva sequence. The accompanying iconography (single uplifted left arm, lotus eyes, no consort) is unique to Shrinathji's pichvai tradition.
